role: Secretary of the Assembly of Experts for the Constitution; IRP central committee member
bio: Hassan Ayat was a lawyer and political activist who served as secretary of the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in 1979. A member of the [Islamic Republican Party](/orgs/islamic-republican-party) central committee, he played a key role in inserting the doctrine of velayat-e faqih into the constitution, arguing forcefully for broad powers for the Supreme Leader. He was elected to the first [Islamic Consultative Assembly](/orgs/islamic-consultative-assembly) (Majles) in 1980. He was assassinated on 5 August 1981, an attack attributed to the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq).
editorial_notes: Born 1938. Assassinated 5 August 1981 by MEK. One of the most vocal advocates for absolute velayat-e faqih in the constitutional assembly debates.
